things.name: Boras Zoo (Djurparken)
things.description: One of Scandinavia's most popular zoos with over 700 animals from 80 species including tigers, gorillas and Scandinavian wildlife

things.name: Textile Museum (Textilmuseet)
things.description: Museum celebrating Boras's heritage as Sweden's textile capital, with historic looms, fashion collections and the story of the Swedish textile industry

things.name: Boras Museum of Art
things.description: Municipal art museum with a strong collection of Nordic modern and contemporary art, including a significant outdoor sculpture collection in the city

things.name: Ramnaparken
things.description: Large forested city park offering walking and cycling trails around the Ramna lakes with abundant bird life and traditional Swedish woodland

things.name: Sandared Sculpture Park
things.description: Boras is famous for its public sculpture trail with over 30 major sculptures by international artists displayed throughout the city centre

things.name: Boras City Church
things.description: Baroque church from 1727 at the heart of the city centre, with an ornate interior and a tall tower that long served as a landmark for travellers

things.name: Viskadalen Valley
things.description: Scenic valley along the Viskan river south of Boras with textile mill heritage, walking trails and the historic mill town of Seglora

things.name: Fristadsparken
things.description: Central city park with the distinctive Fristadsklockstapeln bell tower and open green spaces popular with locals for relaxation

things.name: Boras market (Knalleland)
things.description: One of Sweden's largest retail and factory outlet areas, reflecting Boras's mail order and textile commerce heritage dating to the early 20th century

things.name: Elfsborg Arena
things.description: Home of IF Elfsborg, one of Sweden's most successful clubs with multiple Allsvenskan titles and European competition experience
